reports.text:
Sector wise weightage of Global Markets - India remains the most diversified market in MSCI Index

India Has the Lowest Sector Concentration Among Major Markets with top 3 sector constitute only 52% of MSCI India Index. 

Other Global markets:
Taiwan: 96%
Korea: 86%
Brazil: 68%
China: 64%
Japan: 60%
USA: 58%

India's market is spread across Financials, IT, Industrials, Consumer, Healthcare, Materials, Energy and other sectors.

Over the past 1 year, AI trade has benefitted Taiwan & Korea, not India. A large part is because global capital chased Semiconductors, AI Infrastructure and Hardware Supply Chains. 

Returns:
Korea: 120%
Taiwan: 92%
EM: 42%
India: -13%

Foreign investors have favored AI-linked markets where technology weights are extremely high.

Taiwan IT weight: 88%
Korea IT weight: 61%
USA IT weight: 35%
India IT weight: 8%

Long-term performance still remains strong though India's growth has come down in past 1 year from May'21 to May'26 in USD terms

May'21 - May'25 - Growth: 8%
May'21 - May'26 - Growth: 4%

Any reversal or moderation in AI led trade could significantly benefit the diversified markets such as India.

IDFC FIRST BANK: KPMG FORENSIC REVIEW CONFIRMS CHANDIGARH FRAUD 🚨🏦

• 🔍 KPMG forensic review confirmed fraudulent activities at the Sector 32, Chandigarh branch

• 👥 Fraud involved collusion between certain employees/ex-employees, employees of government departments and third parties

• 📍 Incident was isolated to a single branch; no similar cases found across other branches

• 💰 Net principal amount involved remains around ₹646 Cr, in line with earlier disclosures

• ✅ Bank has already repaid the concerned government departments along with applicable interest and accounted for it in Q4 FY26

• 🖥️ Core Banking System (CBS) records were found to be accurate, with customer balances and transactions properly reflected

• 📧 Customers received regular account statements and transaction alerts as per process

• 🛡️ Bank has implemented enhanced controls including centralized oversight, stronger customer communication and technology-led monitoring systems

• 🚔 Bank stated it is a victim of the fraud and continues to cooperate with investigative authorities

🟡 Impact: Neutral (No increase in financial impact versus earlier disclosures, incident remains isolated and control framework has been strengthened, though governance concerns persist.)

• ♻️ Initiative supports the company's sustainability goals while lowering long-term energy costs

🟢 Impact: Positive (Captive renewable power can reduce energy expenses, improve operational efficiency and strengthen ESG credentials over the long term.)

✈️ **Flywings Simulator Training Centre Ltd. continues to strengthen its position in India's rapidly growing aviation sector.**

✅ FY26 Revenue up 21% YoY to ₹24.51 Cr

✅ EBITDA up 16% YoY to ₹16.11 Cr

✅ PAT up 6% YoY to ₹11.43 Cr

✅ Strong EBITDA Margin of 65.72%

💬 **Management Outlook:**
Management believes Flywings is evolving from a specialized training provider into an integrated aviation training and infrastructure platform. With India's aviation sector witnessing robust growth, increasing demand for trained pilots and crew, DGCA approvals in place, and strategic investments in Full Flight Simulators and new training infrastructure, the company is well positioned to capitalize on long term industry opportunities and create sustainable value for stakeholders.

BHEL: Wins ₹21,000 crore EPC order from Meja Urja Nigam for 3x800 MW thermal power project in UP.

CAREER POINT EDUTECH: Secures ₹1.5 crore government contract from VANARTI for coaching services.

UNITED DRILLING TOOLS: Bags ₹16.61 lakh order from ShivGanga Drillers for drilling stabilizers.

📍 ACQUISITION

JUNIPER HOTELS: To acquire 100% stake in Juniper Hospitality Assets for Dwarka 5-star hotel project.

KV TOYS: To acquire 50% stake in Play Panda for up to ₹4.5 crore to expand in educational toys segment.

📍 COLLABORATION

ASIAN ENERGY SERVICES: Empanelled by Oil India for 3 years for seismic data acquisition services across India.

📍 NEW PROJECTS

JK CEMENT: Secures mining lease for Mahan Coal Mine in MP to strengthen fuel security.

📍 FUNDRAISING

JET FREIGHT: Approves ₹67.36 crore preferential warrant issue to promoters and investors.

DECCAN GOLD MINES: Secures ₹40 crore debt funding from GPIL for Kyrgyzstan gold project development.

📍 CREDIT RATINGS

IDEAFORGE: India Ratings assigns IND BBB (Stable) rating with strong order book visibility.

RAJRATAN GLOBAL WIRE: ICRA assigns A+ (Stable) / A1 rating to ₹50 crore bank facilities.

MARUTI SUZUKI: LIC increases stake to 5% from 4.98% via open market purchase.

GROWW: Friale Fund IV sells 1.13 crore shares; Goldman Sachs Europe buys equal stake at ₹185.5.

GO DIGIT: Peak XV sells stake; Aditya Birla Sunlife MF and JP Morgan funds buy shares at ₹300.

INVENTURUS KNOWLEDGE: Scott Hayworth sells shares; Kedaara Capital buys equivalent stake.

📍 Q2 / BUSINESS UPDATE

SHYAM METALICS: Reports strong YoY growth in pig iron and CR products; mixed performance across segments in May.

TVS MOTOR: HLX motorcycle brand crosses 5 million global sales milestone across 57+ countries.

📍 USFDA / REGULATORY

ALEMBIC PHARMA: Receives USFDA approval for Haloperidol tablets in multiple strengths.

📍 CLARIFICATION

TATA STEEL: Says Port Talbot fire contained; assessing damage with operations expected to resume soon.

RAJESH EXPORTS: Issues clarification on SEBI interim order, denies fraud allegations and assures compliance.

📍 LAUNCHES

GLENMARK: Launches Lacosamide Injection in U.S., expanding injectable portfolio.

HERITAGE FOODS: Launches Creamy Shakes and frozen paneer products on 35th foundation day.

📍 INCORPORATE / CORPORATE STRUCTURE

ANDHRA CEMENTS: To merge with Sagar Cements to simplify structure and improve efficiencies.

ORCHID PHARMA: NCLT approves amalgamation of Dhanuka Laboratories with the company.

PARK MEDI WORLD: Divests 55% stake in Devina Derma, exiting non-core subsidiary.

📍 IT NEWS

📍 OTHERS

BAJAJ ELECTRICALS: Enters cables segment to diversify product portfolio.

COAL INDIA: Eases coal supply norms and boosts linkage auctions to improve availability.

OIL INDIA: Reports second natural gas discovery in Andaman offshore block.

BHARAT COKING COAL: Introduces diesel price relief mechanism for contractors.

TATA COMMUNICATIONS: Reports fire incident at Delhi facility; no casualties and operations remain stable.

TATA INVESTMENTS / TATA CHEMICALS: Fresh complaint filed with Charity Commissioner over Tata Trusts meeting.

reports.text:
"There are certain years in company's journey where performance improves, and there are certain years where foundation for next decade is built. 

For Shilpa Medicare, FY26 has been one such year."

— Keshav Bhutada, CEO

Q4 FY26 , Concall .
END

Final crux:

Shilpa's transformation is underway.

What was once an oncology API company is now evolving into a multi-platform specialty pharma business, with investments made over the last decade beginning to enter the monetization phase.

Mgmt :
"We see a clear path towards sustainable growth, stronger margins and increased return ratios in coming years."

Guidance: 👉constructive on FY27

While avoiding numerical guidance, mgmt's commentary was positive.

Expectations for FY27:
✅ Steady API growth.
✅ Good formulation growth.
✅ Healthy Europe growth.
✅ Continued CDMO momentum.
✅ Similar level of licensing income.

Margin expansion remains an aspiration.

🗣"35% is an ambitious target, which we would be working towards achieving."

Mgmt : 
"We don't project any meaningful contribution coming from OLC in FY27." 

Mgmt: 👉
"We have been growing since past 2, 3 years at a very healthy rate, and we expect that trajectory to continue." 🔥

Biologics and ADCs are entering the monetization phase🔥

Biologics revenues doubled to ₹150 Cr in FY26.

Progress across the platform:
✅ Aflibercept launch targeted in FY27.
✅ Nivolumab entered clinical stage.
✅ Four new biosimilars added.
✅ Two NBE programs entering Phase I studies in FY27.

Shilpa also completed development of its first ADC biosimilar using in-house:

• Payload
• Linker
• Conjugation

The company is now building a fully integrated ADC manufacturing platform.

"With that integrated manufacturing capability, it will be one of its kind in India"💡

NorUDCA is shaping up into a major franchise

Shilpa became the first company globally to obtain approval for NorUDCA in NAFLD.

The company has partnered with 3 leading players in India and also launched its own brand, Noduca™.

Global expansion has begun.

Mgmt highlighted strong clinical outcomes and increasing traction.

"The product is really doing good, and we see very good clinical results in the patient."

More clarity on long-term trajectory is expected after completion of the six-month treatment cycle.

Formulations are emerging as the biggest growth engine

FY26 formulation revenues reached ₹618 Cr (+30%).

Excluding licensing income:
🔹 Q4 growth: 64%
🔹 FY26 growth: 75%

European formulation revenues crossed ₹200 Cr.

Upcoming launches include:
✅ Rotigotine Patch (Europe) – FY27
✅ Ondansetron ER Injection – FY27
✅ Enzalutamide filing – FY27
✅ Abraxane launch – FY28

Mgmt expects healthy growth to continue.

"There is likely to be a good growth possibility in FY27 in the formulation division." 🔥

APIs remain the backbone while CDMO is  becoming meaningful

API revenues grew 16% to ₹985 Cr.

Key developments:
🔹 15+ new oncology APIs added.
🔹 New oncology block under expansion.
🔹 OLC block commissioned.
🔹 Semaglutide validation completed.
🔹 Peptide capacity expansion underway.
🔹 New global clients added.

Importantly, CDMO has already become a sizeable business:💡

🔹 API CDMO revenue ~₹110 Cr
🔹 Biologics CDMO revenue is of similar scale

"Overall, API business is likely to have a steady growth in FY27."🔥

FY26:
🔹 Revenue: ₹1,549 Cr (+18%)
🔹 EBITDA: ₹445 Cr (+30%)
🔹 EBITDA Margin: 29%
🔹 Adj. PAT: ₹232 Cr (+135%)

Adjusted ROCE (excluding biologics/NBE investments) improved sharply from 4% in FY23 to 17.4% in FY26

Mgmt :
"We are on track for a stronger FY27."

Shilpa Medicare Q4 FY26: Building the foundation for the next decade 👉

Shilpa is no longer a single vertical.
It is building a pharma platform spanning:

✅ APIs
✅ Complex Formulations
✅ CDMO
✅ Biologics
✅ ADCs
✅ Recombinant Human Albumin

• 🔄 Wholly-owned subsidiary Aggarwal Hospital & Research Services Pvt Ltd approved divestment of its entire 55% stake in Devina Derma Pvt Ltd 

• 💰 Transaction value stands at ₹0.06 Cr and is expected to be completed on 5 June 2026 

• ❌ Post completion, Devina Derma will cease to be a subsidiary of Park Medi World 

• ⚠️ Divestment undertaken due to non-fulfilment of certain pre-requisite conditions by sellers, which could have impacted operational efficiency and financial flexibility of the group 

• 📊 Devina Derma reported FY26 turnover of ₹0.45 Cr, contributing only 0.03% of consolidated turnover, and had negative net worth of ₹0.34 Cr 

• 🤝 Stake to be sold to Zen Zone Healthcare Pvt Ltd, which is not part of the promoter group 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Exit from a loss-making, non-core subsidiary simplifies the corporate structure and allows management to focus on core healthcare operations.)

DECCAN GOLD MINES : SECURES ₹40 CR FUNDING FROM GPIL ⛏️💰

• 🤝 Deccan Gold Mines has completed documentation for ₹40 crore debt funding from Godawari Power & Ispat Ltd (GPIL). 

• 🌍 Funds will be primarily used for completion and development of the Altyn Tor Gold Project in Kyrgyz Republic through subsidiary Avelum Partner LLC, along with working capital and project development needs. 

• 📅 Loan tenure is 12 months with an interest rate of 12% p.a. (quarterly compounding). 

• 🔒 Deccan Gold has pledged 5 lakh shares of Geomysore Services (India) Pvt Ltd as security for the funding. 

• 🚫 Transaction is not a related-party transaction and GPIL has no shareholding in Deccan Gold. 

• 🏗️ Funding provides near-term capital support for advancing the company's flagship international gold mining project. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens funding position and supports development of the Altyn Tor Gold Project, a key value driver for Deccan Gold.)

RAJESH EXPORTS ISSUES CLARIFICATION ON SEBI INTERIM ORDER 💎📢

• 🏦 Company stated it is debt-free and does not depend on external financing for operations.

• 💰 Said it has not raised funds from public markets after its 1995 IPO of ₹10 crore.

• 📊 Asserted that all financial filings and reported revenues are genuine and accurately disclosed.

• ⚖️ Clarified that the SEBI order is interim in nature and contains observations/suspicions, with no final adverse findings yet.

• 🌍 Explained that a significant portion of consolidated revenue comes from Valcambi, its global gold refining business.

• ❌ Rejected media and social media allegations regarding fraud, inflated revenues and related speculation.

• 📝 Company said it is submitting explanations, documents and evidence to address concerns raised by SEBI.

• 🤝 Management expressed confidence that SEBI will review its submissions and clarify the issues raised in the interim order.

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Clarification may reassure investors, but regulatory uncertainty remains until SEBI issues its final findings.)

reports.text:
OIL INDIA REPORTS SECOND NATURAL GAS DISCOVERY IN ANDAMAN OFFSHORE BLOCK 🛢️🌊

• 🔍 Oil India has reported the presence of natural gas in its 3rd exploratory well (Vijayapuram-3) in the Andaman Shallow Offshore Block.

• 📈 This marks the second hydrocarbon discovery in the block, after gas was found in Vijayapuram-2 in September 2025.

• 🔥 Initial production testing confirmed gas presence through continuous flaring and production flow.

• 🧪 Further sampling and isotope studies are underway to assess gas quality, composition and commercial potential.

• 🌍 OIL has completed acquisition of an additional 600 sq km of 3D seismic data and is processing the data for further exploration.

• ⛏️ Based on seismic interpretation, the company plans to drill an appraisal well to evaluate the discovery.

• 🚀 Discovery strengthens the hydrocarbon potential of the Andaman basin and supports future exploration strategy.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Another gas discovery enhances exploration success, improves resource potential and could create long-term value if commercial reserves are established.)

COAL INDIA EASES COAL SUPPLY NORMS, BOOSTS AVAILABILITY FOR INDUSTRY ⛏️⚡

• 📦 CIL to offer a record 35 MT of high-GCV coal under linkage auctions on 12 June, aimed at reducing imports by sponge iron producers.

• 🏭 Steel (coking) sector allowed to sell surplus coal middlings in the open market, improving operational flexibility.

• 📊 CIL offered 13.75 MT of coal to the steel (coking) sub-sector in the current linkage tranche.

• 🤝 Consortium partner change limit increased from 2 times to 5 times during the linkage period.

• 🏗️ Greenfield and brownfield projects can secure coal linkages before commissioning, helping improve project financing and bankability.

• ⚡ CIL to auction around 34 MT of coal under the SHAKTI policy on 8 June for power sector consumers.

• 🔋 CIL said coal supply to the power sector remains adequate and current stock movements are not a concern.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Higher coal availability, reduced import dependence, and greater flexibility benefit steel, sponge iron, power and industrial consumers.)

GODAWARI POWER & ISPAT (GPIL) : INCREASES INVESTMENT IN BESS SUBSIDIARY 🔋⚡

• 🏭 GPIL has infused an additional ₹100 Crore into its wholly-owned subsidiary Godawari New Energy Pvt Ltd (GNEPL) through a rights issue.

• 📈 Total investment in GNEPL has increased from ₹350 Crore to ₹450 Crore.

• 🔋 Funds will be utilized for setting up a 20 GWh Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) plant and related working capital requirements.

• 📝 GPIL received allotment of 10 crore equity shares of ₹10 each on a rights basis, maintaining 100% ownership.

• 🌱 GNEPL is developing the BESS project in Chhattisgarh, with operations planned in Maharashtra.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Reinforces GPIL's commitment to the fast-growing energy storage segment and strengthens its long-term renewable energy diversification strategy.)

DAM CAPITAL ON RBI MPC & BANKING SECTOR 🏦

• 🔹 No major banking-specific regulatory announcements were made in the MPC policy.

• 💵 FCNR(B) deposit scheme details remain unclear; key question is whether banks will pass on RBI's hedging benefit to deposit rates.

• 🌍 Concessional forex swap facility for PSU ECB borrowings could help support credit growth and lower overseas borrowing costs.

• 💧 RBI announced no additional liquidity measures despite the policy easing.

• 📈 Government's tax exemption on G-Secs, along with RBI measures to attract foreign flows, could stabilize bond yields over time and keep them aligned with the repo rate.

🟢 Impact: Neutral to Positive for Banks (Stable bond yields and support for capital inflows are positives, though absence of fresh liquidity or banking-specific measures limits the immediate impact.)

PHYSICSWALLAH Says 

Will Not Do On-Book Lending With NBFC Entity 

We Are Evaluating Multiple Ways With NBFC Entity

Finance Min says 

-Govt opens Portfolio Investment Scheme (PIS) to all individual Persons Resident Outside India (PROIs).

-Individual PROI investment limit in listed companies raised to 10% from 5%.

-Overall investment limit for all individual PROIs increased to 24% from 10%.

-Govt expands Fully Accessible Route (FAR) to include 15-year, 30-year and 40-year G-Secs.

-Sovereign Green Bonds included under Fully Accessible Route.

-Govt removes short-term investment limit for FPIs in government securities.

-Govt removes concentration limit for FPIs in government securities.

-Govt removes security-wise limit for FPIs investing in government securities.

-Overall FPI investment cap retained at 6% for Central G-Secs and 2% for State G-Secs.

-Govt merges 'general' and 'long-term' FPI investment limits for government securities.

-Govt exempts FPIs from tax on interest income from government securities.

-Govt exempts FPIs from tax on capital gains from government securities.

-Tax exemption for FPIs on G-Secs effective from April 1, 2026.

-Similar tax exemption on G-Secs extended to Bank for International Settlements (BIS)

SHYAM METALICS : MAY 2026 SALES UPDATE 🔥🏭

• 🐷 Pig Iron volume surged 136% YoY to 1.00 lakh MT; realizations up 17.4% YoY. Growth driven by the new Jamuria blast furnace commissioned in Nov'24. 

• 📈 CR Coil/Sheets volume jumped 73.3% YoY to 15,062 MT; realizations increased 19.2% YoY. Color-coated products continue to scale up. 

• 🥇 Stainless Steel sales volume grew 5.3% YoY while realizations rose sharply 32.1% YoY. 

• 📦 Aluminium Foil volumes declined 24.6% YoY, but realizations increased 34.7% YoY. 

• ⚙️ Speciality Alloys volumes fell 18.5% YoY, though realizations improved 26.5% YoY. 

• 🪨 Pellet volumes declined 11.9% YoY; realizations increased 5.1% YoY. 

• 🔩 Carbon Steel volumes were down 3.8% YoY, while realizations rose 2.3% YoY. 

• 🔥 Sponge Iron volumes dropped 44.7% YoY, with realizations up 6.5% YoY.

• ✅ Alembic Pharmaceuticals received USFDA final approval for Haloperidol Tablets USP in strengths of 1 mg, 2 mg, 5 mg, 10 mg and 20 mg.

• 🔄 Product is therapeutically equivalent to Haldol Tablets of Ortho McNeil.

• 🏥 Drug is used for treatment of psychotic disorders and management of Tourette’s syndrome symptoms.

• 💰 According to IQVIA, the product has an estimated U.S. market size of US$27 million for the 12 months ended March 2026.

• 📈 Alembic now has a cumulative 240 ANDA approvals from USFDA, including 221 final approvals.

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Alembic's U.S. generics portfolio and provides an additional revenue opportunity in the U.S. market.)

reports.text:
CITI ON HDFC ASSET MANAGEMENT

Maintains NEUTRAL rating; target price ₹2,800

HDFC Mutual Fund has imposed temporary restrictions on lump sum subscriptions for HDFC Gold ETF and HDFC Gold ETF Fund of Funds (FoF).

Direct lump sum subscriptions of ₹25 crore or more in HDFC Gold ETF are restricted effective June 8, 2026

Lump sum and switch-in transactions for the HDFC Gold ETF FOF are capped at 10 lakh per PAN per month, effective June 5, 2026

Citi characterizes the impact on HDFC AMC's overall revenues as negligible, estimating the contribution from incremental gold ETF flows at less than 1%

Restrictions were implemented in response to broader economic and market conditions to manage inflows efficiently

The firm continues to monitor the redemption trajectory as a key indicator of market sentiment

reports.text:
MOTILAL OSWAL ON INDIAN ECONOMY 

Economy Note - FY27 Consumption Risk and opportunities

El Nino (92-96% probability during core monsoon) + West Asia energy shock = a convergence not priced by markets. 

MOFSL sees FY27 CPI at 5.7% vs RBI's 4.6% — a 110-120bp gap.

Rural India bears the brunt (food is 42% of rural CPI vs 30% urban). The earnings hit lands in corporate results Q1/Q2FY27 well before it shows in GDP prints. 

Urban premium consumption is the hide; while 

Tractors, Entry 2W, Rural FMCG, and MFIs are the pain trade.

Key Monitorable ahead is Reservoir level to assess how Rabi crops will be.

*Morgan Stanley on Titan*
Maintains OVERWEIGHT rating with a ₹5,182 target price
Projects 19% annual jewellery revenue growth through FY30
Aiming to increase market share from 8.5% to 11%
Gold exchange programs now drive 50% of revenue: despite stricter import documentation, supply remains secure
Jewellery demand saw brief impact after Prime Minister's comments but rebounded; plain gold opportunity remains large
Plans to double its Eyecare market share by FY30, targeting 100 new store openings in FY27

*Goldman Sachs on Titan*
Maintains BUY rating; target price of ₹5,400
Projects consolidated revenue and EBIT to grow at ~20% annually through FY26-30, effectively doubling in the period
Core jewellery brands (Tanishq, Mia, Zoya) expected to sustain 20% annual revenue growth, with EBIT growth in the high teens due to mix headwinds
Anticipates strong margin expansion across Caratlane and international operations, including Damas
Forecasts Eyewear and Watches segments to grow faster than the jewellery business, with concurrent margin expansion
Highlights Titan as one of India's fastest-growing consumer companies, noting its consistent performance despite facing multiple headwinds

*CLSA on Titan*
O-P, TP Rs 5249
Reaffirming a 20% Cagr, targeting 2x FY26 revenue & 1.9x EBIT for jewellery segment by FY30
Management noted resilient consumer sentiment
Co’s portfolio-led approach across categories is enabling premiumisation and strengthening brand equity.
Given strong positioning to benefit from rising affluence and premium trends, maintain O-P

*HSBC on Titan*
Buy, TP Rs 5250
Titan’s FY30 ambition implies 19% CAGR each in consol revenues / EBIT and TMZ (Tanishq, Mia, Zoya) at 19% / 16%
Jewellery business growth, following strong three years, looks slightly aggressive, but raise expectation to 17% CAGR
FY27/28e EPS up 1%/2%; beyond short-term regulatory uncertainty, investment case is compelling

*Jefferies on Titan*
Hold, TP Rs 4800
Titan targets c20% revenue/Ebit CAGR over FY26-30, led by SSS, network expansion, formalisation, share gain, premiumisation, etc., notwithstanding ST concerns on macro, inflation & high gold prices.
Cross-sell is a lever with a 50mn customer base and an improving digital/CRM platform.
International also adds to growth
Find Titan mgmt & execution best-in-class, regulatory overhang tempers outlook

*CLSA on Bharti Airtel*
O-P, TP Rs 2310
IPO for Airtel Money, Bharti Airtel’s Africa mobile money business, is scheduled for 2H26.
Airtel Money reportedly could raise US$1.5-2bn at a potential valuation of US$10bn, up four fold from 2021, implying c.60% of Airtel Africa’s market cap
Airtel Money’s FY26 revenue was up 36% YoY to US$1.4bn, while Ebitda increased 31% YoY to US$689m.
Airtel Money penetration is low at 29% of Airtel Africa’s own mobile subscribers of 184m as Nigeria has yet to ramp up; thus, strong growth will likely continue.
Airtel Money business contributes c.20% of region, & Africa accounts for 25% of Bharti Airtel’s consolidated operations.

Quarter-to-date cargo volumes are at 91.4 million tonnes, up 15% YoY and exceeding analyst expectations
Thermal coal handling is rebounding and expected to remain strong through the summer
May logistics rail volumes were 48,170 container units, reflecting a 19% YoY decline
Key growth drivers include Tata Power-linked coal at Mundra, the Vizhinjam transshipment ramp-up, liquid cargo at Mundra, and multimodal logistics parks
Earnings estimates and target price revised upward due to strong volume momentum and improving return on capital employed (ROCE)

Buy, TP Rs 2160
Adani Ports remains confident of achieving its 1bnt cargo target by 2030 (16% CAGR vs JEFe’s 13%), driven by organic growth in domestic ports and existing international port capacity. Integrated logistics is a growth enabler for ports as well.
Capital allocation remains a key focus area with 100 bps annual RoCE expansion target over FY26-31E vs. JEFe 60 bps
Believe strong cash flows enable growth capex, & potential deleveraging to coexist

*JPM on Maruti*
OW. TP Rs 16415
Placed MSIL on Positive Catalyst Watch in April driven by
1) belief that first-time buyer (FTB) segment should continue to revive in the aftermath of the GST cuts;
2) MSIL, having a disproportionate share in the segment, should be a beneficiary
3) MSIL’s outstanding order book and new capacity ramp-up should enable it to gain market-share
As expected, MSIL’s Apr-May 2026 volumes have recovered sharply with wholesale/retail growth at 38%/22% YoY
Its retail market-share has also expanded (120bps YoY to 40%).
However, stock has underperformed the Nifty Auto by 4% since the beginning of FY27
Believe this is driven by fears of commodity driven margin compression
Thesis is that margins could bottom in 1HFY27 (at c6.8% vs. FY26 at 8.4%) & improve to 7.7%/8.5% in 2HFY27/FY28
Current stock price is discounting FY28 EBIT margin of 6.1%.

*Nomura on M&M*
Buy, TP Rs 4580
Strong growth outlook intact
Capacity & model cycle to drive growth
Asia Conf takeaways
UVs: M&M is optimistic on demand outlook of mid-to-high teens volume growth for SUVs in FY27E. Most of models have high demand, but supplies have been affected over past
two months
M&M expects this issue to be resolved in coming months
M&M expects SUV capacity to expand from 64.5k/mth (56.5k ICE+ 8k EVs ) to 68k by Sep-26 & 82k by Mar-26 (70k ICE + 12k EVs)
Launch of NU-IQ platform will be supported by this capacity expansion
Co plans to launch 10 ICEs and 6BEVs over FY27-31E
Nagpur plant will eventually have capacity of 500k SUVs & 100k tractors which will commence operation in 2028

*Nomura on Uno Minda*
Buy TP Rs 1494
Healthy growth outlook intact
Market share gain in existing segments, new customers in new segments, strong potential export growth levers
Asia Forum takeaways
Management noted that near-term demand outlook remains healthy from its customers with no impact from recent production disruptions
While in medium term it continues to expect co to grow 1.5x of industry, rising share of entry-variant sales (where content per vehicle is relatively lower) has impacted Uno Minda’s near-term value growth

*JPM on IT*
Channel checks with industry participants and companies suggest that 1Q could be softer than current expectations, which could drive USD revenue cuts (50-100bps) even as EPS might be protected due to FX
Checks indicate there have been delays in deal ramp-ups and signings due to continued geopolitics-led uncertainty that is keeping clients indecisive.
With a soft 1Q, ask rate for rest of year becomes tougher as the traditional seasonal 1H strength is unlikely to play out, which could drive a slowdown in FY27
That said, despite a soft start to the year, companies may not cut FY27 revenue growth guidance as they still harbor hopes of a 2Q-led recovery, increasing the burden of growth on quarter
In this context, IT stocks are likely to remain volatile & highly reactive to AI news flow over fundamentals
Remain selective & prefer Tech Mahindra/Infosys in large caps and Coforge/Persistent in midcaps.

*CLSA on Coforge*
High Conviction O-P, TP Rs 2075
NDR takeaways
Management was confident in delivering robust growth in FY27 & co would continue to gain market share driven by strong sales rigour and execution focus leading to a healthy deal pipeline. They also highlighted being ahead of curve in terms of infusing AI into delivery and commercial constructs which will continue to drive margins higher in FY27
Successful integration of Encora is on track with revenue synergy expected to be reflected from 3QFY27 onwards
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
*Citi on HDFC Asset Management*
Maintains NEUTRAL rating; target price ₹2,800
Hdfc Mutual Fund has imposed temporary restrictions on lump sum subscriptions for Hdfc Gold ETF and Hdfc Gold ETF Fund of Funds (FoF).
Direct lump sum subscriptions of ₹25 crore or more in Hdfc Gold ETF are restricted effective June 8, 2026
Lump sum and switch-in transactions for the Hdfc Gold ETF FoF are capped at ₹10 lakh per PAN per month, effective June 5, 2026
Citi characterizes the impact on Hdfc AMC's overall revenues as negligible, estimating the contribution from incremental gold ETF flows at less than 1%
Restrictions were implemented in response to broader economic and market conditions to manage inflows efficiently
The firm continues to monitor the redemption trajectory as a key indicator of market sentiment

*Axis Capital on Voltas*
Recommendation: Accumulate; Target: ₹1,471, Earlier Target: ₹1,442
Thrust on volume plus market share gain; margins to follow
Sharper focus on market share plus volumes led by aggressive pricing
Inhouse strategy has led Voltas gain ~170bps market share YoY in Apr'26
Expect it to post industry-leading volume growth in Q1

*Citi on Max Healthcare*
Recommendation: Accumulate; Target: ₹1,330
Company is targeting 10-15% annual bed additions, primarily brownfield-led (~60%)
Execution delays (~4-6 months) are temporary and not margin-dilutive
Near-term margins may remain impacted by pre-hiring and commissioning costs
Insurance related issues are largely behind with the mgmt

*Citi on Sun Pharma*
Recommendation: Accumulate, Target: ₹2,180
In Organon specialty portfolio, VTAMA and Emgality are witnessing strong growth
Organon’s strong presence in China provides a meaningful opportunity
Sun can also commercialize its own products across markets such as China and Korea
Biosimilars and established brands offer additional growth levers

*Citi on ICICI Lombard*
Expect gross premium growth to be 100-200 bps higher than industry average
Competitive pricing in commercial lines
IFRS can lead to marginal improvement in profitability in short-term
Focus on gradual improvement in combined ratio

*Citi on Grasim*
Recommendation: Accumulate; Target: ₹3,600
Paints—price hikes, cost pressures, uncertainty beyond 1Q, key priorities for Birla Opus
Capital allocation—focus on core businesses now following two new businesses
Stronger sequential 1Q for VSF and Chemicals
Paint revenue market share likely crossed ~10% in Mar26

*Citi on GAIL*
Recommendation: Accumulate; Target: ₹195
Transmission volumes have recovered from the lows of March 2026
Mgmt. is confident of meeting its gas marketing EBIT guidance of Rs40-45bn in FY27E
LPG segment is expected to deliver improved profitability
Petchem plant is still just running at half load, losses could narrow given higher realisations

*Citi on Lupin*
Recommendation: Accumulate; Target: ₹2,540
Confident of maintaining ~25% EBITDA margin in FY27 as well as in FY28E
India business is expected to grow at 1.2-1.3x IPM in FY27
US sales are expected to be US$1-1.1bn in FY27E, followed by further growth in FY28E
Net debt is unlikely to exceed 2x EBITDA even in the event of an acquisition

*Citi on Eicher Motors*
Recommendation: Accumulate; Target: ₹8,350
Domestic demand remains strong with enquiries growing at healthy levels
Wholesale volumes were impacted by capacity constraints
Raw material costs rose by 33.5% (% of revenue) between March and May
Capacity expansion from 1.45mn to 2mn annual units remains on track

*Morgan Stanley on L&T*
Recommendation: Overweight; Target: ₹4,968
F27 outlook: 1HF27 would be subdued & Hormuz should open by Sept or Oct-27
F31 likely revenue mix: core 60%, new business 20%, services 20%
Middle East: No order slowdown/cancellations
Qatar seeing pauses, with logistics cost pass-through under discussion

reports.text:
*Mahesh Nandurkar – Jefferies*
Model Portfolio changes
Reduce NBFC weight by reducing Chola (CV exposure amid fuel price hikes)
In autos, replace TVS & M&M (margin pressure, weak rural outlook) with Eicher (better pricing power given premium positioning)
Add Premier (14x Mar'27 EV/ Ebitda), a play on renewable energy security with 33% EBITDA CAGR over FY26-28E
Also add Adani Ports (hard asset play) by trimming Adani Energy
Replace Ultratech with Grasim on improving standalone (paints + chemicals) business which will likely help narrow holdco disc. (current: 35-40%)
Replace Max FS with Star Health, given stronger 30% IFRS EPS CAGR over FY26-29e (vs 18% VNB CAGR for Max) and lower regulatory risk
Add Groww, which is well-placed to deliver 30% EPS CAGR (FY26-29e) driven by product expansion

*Morgan Stanley on Earnings*
Actionable ideas from Q4FY26
2/3RD of coverage beat expectations
Profit growth remained in double digits for second consecutive quarter
Long-term story is gaining strength with cyclical recovery led by policy action
Overweight on Grasim, Polycab, Prestige, ICICI Bank, Shriram Finance, TVS, and Titan.
Underweight: Dalmia, Havells, Bank of India, L&T Finance,
Underweight: Tata Chemicals, Amara Raja, and Dabur

*Bofa on RBI Preview*
RBI staring at a difficult policy backdrop
A hold for now is likely the most logical choice
Strong arguments are in favor of a hike
Expected to tighten policy, despite not seeing either higher inflation

*Goldman Sachs on RBI Preview* 
Resilient growth in the early phase of the Middle East shock
Expect Q1 CY26 real GDP growth at 7.2% YoY
Headline inflation to increase above 5% in 2H CY26
Ample liquidity but tighter term funding conditions
Expect RBI to remain on hold at its June policy meeting & retain its “neutral” stance
But expect hawkish forward guidance
Bar to hiking interest rates to defend the currency is high
Do expect RBI to announce measures to incentivize dollar inflows

*Citi on India Economics* 
Dual Shocks Raise Inflation risks
Expect June MPC to keep rates on hold but now anticipate 2×25bps hikes in Aug & Oct
Bringing forward rate hike views
Govt’s cumulative fuel price hikes (INR 7.5/liter) exceeded base
Revise FY27 average inflation forecast to 4.9%YY (vs. 4.6% earlier)
Anticipate core inflation at 4.6%YY (vs. 4.4% earlier)
Expect higher seasonal volatility in vegetable prices
Driven by a weaker monsoon and higher input cost passthrough

CLSA on Bharti Airtel

IPO for Airtel Money, Bharti Airtel’s Africa mobile money business, is scheduled for 2H26.

Airtel Money reportedly could raise US$1.5-2bn at a potential valuation of US$10bn, up four fold from 2021, implying c.60% of Airtel Africa’s market cap
Airtel Money’s FY26 revenue was up 36% YoY to US$1.4bn, while Ebitda increased 31% YoY to US$689m.

Airtel Money business contributes c.20% of region, & Africa accounts for 25% of Bharti Airtel’s consolidated operations.

MORGAN STANLEY ON TITAN

OVERWEIGHT, TARGET 5182

Jewellery demand saw some impact after PM's comments, but rebounded thereafter

Titan continues to promote gold exchange

Jewellery (TMZ) revenues to grow at a 19% F26-F30 CAGR, with a 16% EBIT CAGR

Market share gains to continue (4.5% F19, 8.5% F26, and target 11% by F30)

MOTILAL OSWAL ON INDIAN ECONOMY 

Economy Note - FY27 Consumption Risk and opportunities

El Nino (92-96% probability during core monsoon) + West Asia energy shock = a convergence not priced by markets. 

MOFSL sees FY27 CPI at 5.7% vs RBI's 4.6% — a 110-120bp gap.

Rural India bears the brunt (food is 42% of rural CPI vs 30% urban). The earnings hit lands in corporate results Q1/Q2FY27 well before it shows in GDP prints. 

Urban premium consumption is the hide; while 

Tractors, Entry 2W, Rural FMCG, and MFIs are the pain trade.

Key Monitorable ahead is Reservoir level to assess how Rabi crops will be.

India defence production tripled to 1.5 lakh crore in five years, but the supply chain behind it still has gaps at the component level. 

Every aircraft programme runs on thousands of small commodity parts like fasteners and bearings, low cost high volume, where a missing piece delays the whole line. 

These C-class parts are what integrators like ALA manage, This JV enters at the plumbing level of defence manufacturing, less exciting to talk about but very hard to replace once you are embedded in a customer's process.

The timing of this JV fits a global pattern, Airbus and Boeing face supply chain pressure through at least 2027, short on people, materials and certified vendors, pushing them to find new sources. 

India Tata-Airbus C-295 line is running in Vadodara, L&T builds submarines, Bharat Forge makes artillery. All of them need someone managing the certified parts pipeline. 

TVS has UK defence work worth about 140m dollars a year, ALA brings European OEM contacts. India 75% domestic procurement mandate makes this demand policy-backed, not voluntary.

The question for TVS SCS is not whether defence supply chains are valuable, they are. It is whether this JV changes its margin profile. 

The base logistics business earns under 1% PAT and the stock has not recovered to its 2023 IPO price. Defence supply chains globally earn 8 to 9% PBT because certified customers rarely switch. 

If TVS can shift even a modest share of revenue toward this stickier work over the next five years, the blended economics of the company start to look different.

MASTER COMPONENTS FORMS 50:50 JV WITH PRATHAMESH INDUSTRIES 🏭🤝

• 🏭 Master Components has signed a Joint Venture Agreement with Prathamesh Industries (India) Ltd

• ⚙️ JV will manufacture, process and trade SMC (Sheet Moulding Compound), DMC (Dough Moulding Compound) and other composite materials

• 🇮🇳 Focus on serving both domestic and international markets

• 📊 Shareholding structure: 50% Master Components, 50% Prathamesh Industries

• 💰 Initial paid-up capital of JV: ₹10 lakh (₹5 lakh contribution by each partner)

• 🏢 JV company proposed to be incorporated by 30 September 2026

• 👨‍💼 Board to have equal representation from both partners

• 📈 JV will become an associate company of Master Components

• 🎯 Objective is to expand into composites manufacturing and strengthen presence in industrial materials segment

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strategic diversification into high-growth composites and specialty materials with shared investment risk.)

reports.text:
ANDHRA PAPER: KADIAM UNIT DISRUPTION CONTINUES, PRODUCTION LOSS OF 205 MT/DAY 🏭

• 🏭 Manufacturing operations at the Kadiam Unit (East Godavari, Andhra Pradesh) remain disrupted despite revocation of the lockout

• 👷 Contract workmen have not resumed duties, leading to continued production losses

• 📉 Estimated production loss stands at ~205 MT per day

• 💰 Company estimates a revenue loss of around ₹143 lakh per day (₹1.43 crore/day)

• 🤝 Management is actively engaging with workmen representatives and authorities to resolve the impasse

• 📋 Disruption is linked to demands raised by contract workers regarding revision of contractual terms

• 🛡️ All assets are covered by insurance

• ⏳ Management expects discussions to conclude within a short timeframe and operations to return to normal

🟡 Impact: Negative (Ongoing production disruption and daily revenue loss create near-term earnings pressure until normal operations are restored.)

AUROBINDO PHARMA RECEIVES USFDA APPROVAL FOR TOFACITINIB TABLETS 🇺🇸

• ✅ Received final USFDA approval for Tofacitinib Tablets 5 mg and 10 mg

• 🔬 Generic version of Xeljanz Tablets of Pfizer

• 🏭 Product will be manufactured at APL Healthcare Unit IV, a wholly owned subsidiary

• 🚀 Commercial launch to begin immediately

• 💰 As per IQVIA MAT (Apr 2026), the U.S. market for Tofacitinib Tablets 5 mg & 10 mg is estimated at ~US$494 million

• 📋 Tofacitinib is used for treatment of:Rheumatoid Arthritis
,Psoriatic Arthritis, Ulcerative Colitis

• 📈 Aurobindo now has 586 ANDA approvals from USFDA, comprising:561 Final Approvals, 25 Tentative Approvals

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Aurobindo's U.S. generics portfolio, provides access to a sizeable ~$494 million market and supports future revenue growth.)

Power grids are emerging as the key bottleneck to energy transition. 

BNEF expects sing renewable penetration, data centers and industrial growth to drive ~US$15tn in global T&D capex over CY25-50. 

We see India as uniquely positioned (it manufactures ~80% of the global T&D products) to benefit 
from a large domestic transmission buildout, accelerating HVDC adoption, favourable localization policies, and growing export opportunities.

Maintains BUY rating on InterGlobe Aviation

Government approved 100 billion in budgetary support to Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) for Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) price stabilization

Support facilitates interest-free advances to OMCs to help them absorb losses when global ATF prices exceed set benchmarks

Mechanism aims to provide price predictability and shield airlines from extreme fuel cost volatility caused by the West Asia crisis

Fund operates on a true-up model: OMCs will repay the government when global fuel prices moderate

Analyst scenario analysis indicates the support could cover 2-5 months of fuel volatility, depending on price points

No specific estimate changes were made pending further clarity on the fuel price cap mechanism

India's oil product exports are collapsing as the Iran War forces refiners to keep fuel at home:

India's oil product exports dropped -31% YoY in May, to 878,000 barrels per day, the lowest since October 2022.

This also marks the 3rd consecutive monthly decline, totaling ~270,000 barrels.

By comparison, the 2025 high was ~1,500,000 barrels per day in September, or +70% larger.

This comes as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has severely restricted crude and fuel flows to India, prompting refiners to redirect output toward domestic supply rather than overseas markets.

Indian refiners have also shifted production toward LPG at the expense of other exportable fuels, further weighing on overall outbound shipments.

Before the Iran War, the country imported ~90% of its LPG supply from the Middle East, making domestic production a strategic necessity once Hormuz flows were disrupted.

Domestic energy security is now a top priority in India.

🇺🇸 Trade war is back on the menu, boys.

Trump team just dropped a new plan: 10% tariffs on imports from 60 countries.
→ 10% for Canada, Mexico, EU & UK (the ones trying on forced labor)
→ 12.5% for China, Brazil, India etc.

But don’t worry, coffee, beef, tomatoes & orange juice get a pass so your grocery bill doesn’t explode.

Critics calling it classic goalpost moving again. Inflation still the big headache before midterms.

Source: Yahoo Finance

reports.text:
Kotak Inst Eqt on Cartrade
Upgrade to Buy, TP Raised to Rs 2300

Cartrade stock price has come off sharply YTD on concerns of disruptions from AI models
Believe co’s deep dealer database and value-added offerings may limit the impact to an extent

Upgrade, however, is on account of a sharply higher valuation for OLX as envisage steady GMV growth, improving monetization and margins

Raise FY2027-29 EPS estimates by 1-5% on higher profitability estimates for OLX.

@Stockinfo333

UBS on LTMindtree
Neutral, TP Rs 4520

Analyst Day: AI-led TAM expansion to drive potential doubling of revenues in 5 years
Management currently estimates an enterprise tech spend of US$1.3tn & expects 20-25% of this (US$0.3tn) to shrink in the traditional model.

However, they see rising spend as enterprises hurry to modernise their tech debt (+US$0.5tn) and emergence of Business AI Services (+US$0.8tn) taking total enterprise spending to US$2.3tn.

Further, they stated that while FY26 was year of pilots and POCs, FY27 is expected to be year of AI adoption at scale, creating a large opportunity for companies like LTM

@Stockinfo333

Jefferies on LTMindtree
U-P, TP Rs 3500

LTM indicated that AI-led disruption could deflate core IT service revenues by c.25%, but AI will expand the addressable market by US$1.3tn, driven by Platformized IT and Business AI services.

LTM's plans to pivot to outcomebased offerings require a material change to pricing and talent strategy which may not be easy to execute.

In this context, its aspirations to double revenues by 2031 with 200bps margin expansion seem very optimistic.

Nomura on Adani Ports
Buy, TP Rs 1930

May-26 traffic of 48MT up 16% y-y, as containers/liquid cargo grow 17%/33% y-y

Furthermore, management expects healthy growth momentum until FY31E & has guided for revenue/EBITDA CAGRs of 19%/18%
over FY26-31E.

With a 27% share in India’s port traffic & a cargo handling capacity of 653MT, ADSEZ remains India’s largest private port operator with strong defensible moats such as
industry-leading operating efficiency and integrated logistics offering

Expect ADSEZ to deliver a 19% EBITDA CAGR over FY26-28F with ports/logistics/marine segments recording EBITDA CAGRs of 18%/30%/29%

Jefferies on Adani Ports
Buy, TP Rs 2160

Raise FY28E-29E EBITDA by 3-5% and estimate 15% FY26-31 EBITDA CAGR vs 13% earlier, as find potential for demand-supply gap at Gujarat and Maharashtra Container Ports, driving market share gains for Adani Ports

Vizhinjam offers incremental upside with market share gain potential in the southern markets through lower transit times and costs to EXIM cargo.

Strong cash flows enable growth capex, and potential deleveraging to coexist

@ipoinfo3

Jefferies on Tata Con
Buy TP Rs 1450

AR Analysis
FY26 reflects a clear execution-led inflection

Core categories remained resilient, while growth portfolio scaled meaningfully, driven by premiumisation, health-led innovation & expanding alternate channels.

Importantly, growth quality improved, supported by tighter coordination across innovation, distribution & sourcing
GTM reset and AI-enabled decision-making are now shaping outcomes, improving repeatability & building resilience despite high volatility.

@ipoinfo3

Jefferies on Mankind Pharma
Buy, TP Rs 3000

recently met mgt, it's double-digit India growth should be driven by recovery in acute therapies which is visible in stronger volume growth, continued performance in chronic therapies and wider coverage for BSV products.

High teens export revenue growth in BSV would be driven by deeper market penetration and newer markets.

Over MT-LT, mgt believes investments in R&D, which includes in-house assets could help deliver growth.

@ipoinfo3

Jefferies on Adani Enterprise
Buy, TP Rs 2800

AEL is best understood as an incubation platform rather than conventional conglomerate, building & scaling large biz in sectors where India still faces supply gaps.

In its latest AR, AEL highlights focus on projects in infra, energy transition/AI, & import substitution, capitalising on group's existent positioning in Logistics/Utilities.

FY26 marked a key transition, with multiple projects moving into Ops, setting up FY27 for a more visible earnings scaleup.

Jefferies on Titagarh
Buy, TP raised to Rs 990

Q4 EBITDA was 7% above JEFe driven by better-than-expected execution &margins

Co dispatched 25 metro coaches in Mar-26Q vs 21 in 1HFY26, & management guides for sustained execution ramp-up in FY27E

Believe Titagarh is a key beneficiary of rising passenger & metro coach demand led by government initiatives

Estimate 44% FY26-30E EPS CAGR where strong Passenger segment Order Book lends visibility.

Jefferies on Jupiter Wagons
U-P, TP Rs 210

Q4 EBITDA was 32% below JEFe due to weak wagon sales, partially offset by 19% YoY growth in wheelset sales

Co is a key beneficiary of Indian Railways' indigenisation initiatives on wheel manufacturing and components.

However, its Odisha wheel manufacturing plant is expected to be operational only by end-FY28E, and component manufacturing JVs are still in nascent stages.

HSBC India's New IP Series

April IP rose 4.9% year-on-year beating market expectations; front-loading and inventory build-up supported manufacturing, particularly for consumer goods

The new series shows a stronger industrial growth compared to the old series

However, growth has eased in FY26, led by weaker manufacturing output

Correlations between the new IP and the new GDP manufacturing series have fallen sharply, but could rise back up with subsequent GDP revisions

Nomura On AMC Stocks

Expect INR 313 billion net equity inflow in May-26F

ICICI PRU AMC gets the highest absolute flows, Nippon AMC flow share above AUM share

Nippon AMC continued its strong momentum (flow share at 10.6%, 3.3pp above its AUM share of 7.3%

ICICI Pru AMC flow share (13.9%) is almost at par with its AUM share of 14.1%

HDFC AMC's (flow share at 11.0% has fallen below its AUM share of 12.9% as inflow in flexicap/midcap/balanced advantage schemes moderated

Prefer Nippon AMC for its consistent AUM market share gains followed by HDFC AMC

Emkay On LTM Investor Day

Retain ADD and target price of Rs4,700

Management outlined its 5Y 'Lakshya 2031' strategy

Anchored on repositioning the company from a traditional tech services provider to an Al-led 'Business Creativity Partner

Targets ~2x revenue by FY31, implying a CAGR of ~15% (15-17% contribution from M&As) with ~200bps EBITM expansion

Partnership with Randstad to be value-accretive - no material margin impact on F27

**How to Make Life More Positive:**

1) Smile more
2) Be yourself
3) Be optimistic
4) Don’t complain
5) Throw away clutter
6) Make a change/difference
7) Unfollow negative people on social media
8) Go to bed early & wake up early
9) Show more gratitude
10) Do things that challenge you

Google on AI

“AI is driving an expansionary moment for Alphabet. The company is experiencing strong demand for its AI solutions and services from enterprises and consumers, at levels that are exceeding the company’s available supply.”
In roughly 28 years, Google/Alphabet has tapped primary equity capital only seven times; the 2026 announced $80B raise is more than 10x all prior identified primary raises combined.

Alphabet to raise $80B from share sales to fund AI spending splurge, Berkshire to invest $10B

Very bullish news, as it shows again the limiting factor is no longer demand, models, or products, but compute supply.

For years, the cleanest story was excess cash, buybacks, and software-like returns; now the story is closer to railroads, power grids, and fabs, where whoever can finance the bottleneck may own the toll road.

Berkshire’s $10B placement gives the raise a stamp of patient capital.

Alphabet is not simply selling $80B of common stock straight into the market tomorrow for AI capex. It is using a mix of underwritten stock, mandatory convertible preferred, Berkshire private placement, and a $40B ATM program, with about $30B of that ATM tied to employee equity tax mechanics.

MORGAN STANLEY ON INDIA EQUITY STRATEGY

Neutral stance on India equities

Nifty bottom likely behind us; earnings growth acceleration expected in coming quarters

India earnings seen entering a new upcycle; FY26 earnings growth subdued but recovery expected across multiple sectors

Key near-term risks are prolonged Middle East conflict and severe drought from bad monsoon

Capital spending upcycle expected across energy, defence, semiconductors, fertilizers and data centres; investments to GDP seen rising to 37.5% over five years

Lack of direct Al play and potential Al disruption to services exports flagged as persistent challenge to the market

Portfolio positioning favours domestic cyclicals over defensives; overweight financials, consumer discretionary and industrials

Underweight energy, materials, utilities and healthcare

IT services flagged as potential dark horse as global companies pivot to building Al applications

MACQUARIE ON DIXON TECH

OUTPERFORM, TARGET 15,000

ELARA CAPITAL ON BEML

EBITDA declined 36% year on year due to one-off provisions of around ৳ 1.5bn related to Mumbai metro project

Excluding one-offs EBITDA margin contracted 210 basis points year on year to 23.5%

Order inflows grew 20% year on year to ປີ 12.5 bn in Q4; order book at ৳ 159 bn provides strong revenue visibility

FY27 and FY28 earnings estimates cut 8% and 6% respectively on mining segment slowdown

Earnings growth of 83% expected over FY26-29 with average return on equity of 20%

Valued at 37x March 2028 estimated EPS unchanged

MS || Bottom May Be Behind Us

With earnings growth acceleration likely in the pipeline and valuations and sentiment coming off near extremes, Indian equities appear poised for a strong year ahead.

INVESTEC IN CELLO WORLD

SELL , Cuts Target Price To 435 From 515

Expect downgrades to continue

Opalware continues to see increase in competitive intensity

Glassware faces constant pressure from imports further delaying ramp-up

Receivables rationalization to impact growth and margins

Reduce target multiple to 24x (30x, DCF derived) and PAT ests. are 26/13% lower

MORGAN STANLEY ON INDIA AUTOS

Preferred picks are Maruti Suzuki, Mahindra, TVS and Hero

May 2026 retail sales healthy across passenger vehicles, two-wheelers, light commercial vehicles and tractors

Passenger vehicle and two-wheeler retail sales grew 27% and 11% year on year respectively; tractor retail up 16% year on year

Medium and heavy commercial vehicle growth moderated to 6% year on year; fleet operators under pressure from higher input costs and diesel prices

Electric vehicle penetration improving; two-wheeler electric share at 9.2% in May vs 7.9% in April; passenger vehicle electric share at 6.4% vs 5.9% in April

Mahindra flagged supply chain constraints from manpower shortages at select suppliers; could have added 10% growth in utility vehicles and light commercial vehicles without these

- Chinese steel exports continue to decline

**Aluminium Outlook**
- Global aluminium supply remains tight
- Supportive demand-supply environment

**Coal Outlook**
- Domestic coal availability remains adequate
- Well-supplied market conditions

**Currency Impact**
- INR weakness versus RMB is positive
- Supports competitiveness of Indian producers

**Preferred Picks**
- Hindalco Industries
- NALCO
- Tata Steel
- JSW Steel
- Jindal Stainless
- Hindustan Zinc

**Key Takeaway**
- HSBC remains positive on the sector due to supportive valuations, improving earnings outlook, tight aluminium supply, declining Chinese steel exports and favorable currency dynamics.

**NOMURA ON APAR INDUSTRIES ⚡**

**Rating & Target Price**
- Maintains **BUY** rating
- Target Price raised to **₹14,240** from ₹10,600

**Key Positives**
- Strong Q4FY26 performance
- Outlook remains firm
- Conductor demand remains structurally healthy

**Estimate Revisions**
- FY27 EBITDA estimate raised by 7%
- FY28 EBITDA estimate raised by 9%

**Growth Outlook**
- FY26–FY29 EBITDA CAGR estimated at 19%
- Strong growth expected across key segments

**Cable Business**
- Robust growth momentum continues
- Data Centre (DC) opportunity emerging strongly
- DC projects offer premium pricing
- Additional demand tailwinds expected from AI and digital infrastructure investments

**Key Takeaway**
- Conductor business supported by long-term power infrastructure demand
- Cable segment continues to be a major growth driver
- Data centres provide a new high-value growth opportunity

**CLSA ON INDIAN IT – HOW MUCH OF THE “SAASPOCALYPSE” IS REAL? 🤖💻**

**Key Observations**
- AI is driving a shift in pricing models
- Industry moving from seat-based pricing
- Consumption-based pricing gaining traction

**Industry Outlook**
- Latest guidance remains robust
- EPS expectations continue to be healthy
- No major deterioration visible in demand trends

**Impact On IT Services**
- Strong SaaS partnerships remain an advantage
- Product engineering demand remains healthy
- Implementation-related work continues to see traction
- AI adoption creating new service opportunities

**Companies With Higher SaaS Exposure**
- Wipro among large-cap IT companies
- Persistent Systems among mid-cap IT companies

**Key Takeaway**
- AI is changing SaaS business models rather than eliminating demand
- IT companies with strong SaaS ecosystems and engineering capabilities are expected to benefit from the transition
